items.notes: Next-Gen Antennas product line covers spirals, interferometer arrays, conformal datalink antennas, high-power horns/notches for EW and UAV platforms Groups 1-5.

evidence.snippet: Axillon Aerospace is a leading provider of engineered carbon fiber composite components and fuel containment solutions for the defense and commercial aerospace markets. The business generates annual sales of approximately $400 million, operates six manufacturing locations across the USA and Mexico, and employs approximately 1,700 people. For more information, please visit [www.axillonaerospace.com](https://www.axillonaerospace.com).

headquarters.notes: Site confirms operations across the USA and Mexico; phone number (678) is Georgia area code but no explicit corporate HQ address stated. The fuel-containment division (since sold) was based in Rockmart, GA; corporate HQ for remaining Engineered Composites not explicitly published.

drone_supply_chain_role.notes: Sells finished composite structural components, radomes, antennas, and electro-thermal assemblies that go into aerospace/defense platforms including UAVs Groups 1-5. Does not make complete drone platforms.

ndaa.notes: Site mentions ITAR Compliant, AS9100, NADCAP certifications but no NDAA Section 848/889 claim anywhere in corpus.

blue_uas.notes: No Blue UAS or Blue sUAS mention anywhere in corpus.

Axillon Aerospace is a leading provider of engineered carbon fiber composite components and fuel containment solutions for the defense and commercial aerospace markets. The business generates annual sales of approximately $400 million, operates six manufacturing locations across the USA and Mexico, and employs approximately 1,700 people. For more information, please visit [www.axillonaerospace.com](https://www.axillonaerospace.com).

readiness.notes: ~$400M annual sales, 1,700 employees, five manufacturing sites, NADCAP/AS9100 certified. Full serial production posture.

unresolved_questions: Corporate HQ city/address not explicitly published on website; phone 678 area code suggests Georgia but no confirmation after fuel containment sale.
tagline: Supplier of composite structures, engine components, electro-thermal ice protection, and RF/antenna systems for defense and commercial aerospace. Production-ready across five facilities. NDAA and Blue UAS certification status unknown; public evidence of specific defense programs or platform integration limited.
